body:
This PR restructures the RRTMGP.jl public API to separate concerns, complete the
decoupling from ClimaCore, and enable ClimaAtmos to drop its ~1160-line
`RRTMGPInterface.jl` wrapper. This is breaking—will release (v0.22.0) later.

### Changes

- **Thin orchestrator:** `RRTMGPSolver` is now a lightweight, `Adapt`-compatible
  aggregate over the functional `solve_lw!`/`solve_sw!` core, storing the
  interpolation/extrapolation config, `deep_atmosphere_inverse_scaling`, lookup
  tables, and flux buffers. `update_fluxes!`/`update_net_fluxes!` run an
  allocation-free, type-stable pipeline and return `nothing` (read results via
  getters). `Adapt.adapt_structure` overloads for the optics and source workspaces
  re-alias the packed `layerdata`/`leveldata` scratch into a single adapted buffer
  across a device transfer, so ClimaCoupler checkpoint restores stay zero-allocation.

- **Foolproof grid params:** `RRTMGPGridParams(FT; domain_nlay, ncol, …)` takes the
  *physical* layer count and adds the isothermal boundary layer internally (the stored
  `nlay` is the total), so callers can't miscount. The solver's output-buffer fields
  are `net_flux_buffer`/`clear_net_flux_buffer`, disambiguated from the `net_flux(s)`/
  `clear_net_flux(s)` getters.

- **Standalone API:** Added `solve_gray(FT)` (with `default_parameters` and
  `heating_rate`, in K/s) as a 1D functional front door for classroom use. The gray
  path needs no NetCDF tables.

- **Grid adaptation & interpolation:** Lifted the level interpolation and isothermal
  boundary-layer cascade (`interpolate_levels!`, `add_isothermal_boundary_layer!`,
  `clip!`, `update_concentrations!`) out of ClimaAtmos and directly into RRTMGP. The
  logic operates on plain `(nlay, ncol)` array views, completing the decoupling from
  ClimaCore.

- **Canonical aerosol map:** Introduced `src/api/aerosols.jl` as the single source of
  truth for the aerosol species ordering, including lookup-loading assertions and a
  name-map cross-check test. The API accepts canonical names only (e.g. `sulfate`,
  `sea_salt1`); host models map their own short names (`so4`, `ss1`, …).

- **Full getter API:** Completed the public getter interface so hosts write/read only
  via array views: `layer_pressure`/`layer_temperature`/`layer_relative_humidity`
  (centers), `level_pressure`/`level_temperature` (faces), `net_flux`,
  `radiation_method`, and the cloud/aerosol/flux getters. Getters return `(nlay, ncol)`
  centers / `(nlev, ncol)` faces (physical dim first) as domain-only views (excluding
  the isothermal boundary layer) so a host's arrays match its physical grid. Layer-2
  names follow RT convention (`layer_`/`level_` prefixes, full words `aerosol_*`), and
  the deep-atmosphere getter/kwarg is `deep_atmosphere_inverse_scaling` (the low-level
  `metric_scaling` argument is unchanged).

- **Spectrally-resolved fluxes (optional):** Construct with `spectral_fluxes = true`
  to retain per-band fluxes, read via `spectral_{lw,sw}_flux_{up,dn,net}` and the band
  edges `{lw,sw}_band_bounds`. Off by default (zero cost); the two-stream kernels
  accumulate per band with the broadband result byte-for-byte unchanged
  (`sum(bands) == broadband`).

- **Buildkite fix:** Updated SLURM specs to use `--gpus` (`slurm_gpus: 1`) instead of
  `--gres=gpu` to resolve a job submission failure on `central`.

- **Docs & tests:** New "Functional core" and "The getter contract" pages document
  Layer 1 and the host data-exchange contract; the radiation-method docstrings are
  completed. Docstrings/formatting follow the CliMA DeveloperGuides. Added tests for the
  aerosol name map, grid adaptation, the standalone gray path, boundary-layer-masked
  getters, per-band flux consistency, the Layer-2 zero-allocation/type-stability
  contract, and an `Adapt` round-trip.

- **Cleanup:** Remove Infiltrator (closes #489). Add partial cloud cover test (closes
  #547 and #441). Fix Aqua failures by removing `Base.Array` type-piracy overloads
  (closes #578). Remove the internal `RRTMGPData` datalayout subsystem (unused by the
  getter API), which also drops the `LinearAlgebra` dependency. Remove the 14 deprecated
  positional-argument constructors that warned to use `RRTMGPGridParams`, plus the dead
  flux/`vmr` helper methods. Drop `@fastmath` from the longwave kernels (kept for
  shortwave) so GPU longwave fluxes match CPU at the tight test tolerance.
  
- **Not implemented:** Explored changing the datalayout internally from `(nlev, ncol)` to `(ncol, nlev)` for fluxes, for coalesced memory access. This improved performance by 10-20%. However, since the analogous change cannot be done for optics while keeping array views for the coupling to ClimaAtmos, the API became brittle; this seems ultimately not worth the modest performance gain.

Corresponding host changes: ClimaAtmos CliMA/ClimaAtmos.jl#4624 (phases out the
`RRTMGPModel` wrapper in favor of direct `RRTMGPSolver` usage, now constructing with
`domain_nlay`) and a version bump in ClimaCoupler CliMA/ClimaCoupler.jl#2009 (both pass
tests with this RRTMGP branch).
title: API Redesign: Functional Core, Standalone API, and ClimaCore Decoupling (#1 in refactor chain)
